============================================================
RAW DATA UNAVAILABLE
The following expected raw data files are missing from /workspace/raw_data/:
  - artists.csv
  - directors.csv
  - scientists.csv
Therefore, the quantitative results cannot be reproduced from the original data.
Below are the key numerical results as reported in the paper (PAPER_REPORTED).
============================================================
RESULT colocation_ratio_artists    = 1.48  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT colocation_ratio_directors  = 1.42  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT colocation_ratio_scientists = 1.57  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_at_least_one_hot_streak_artists   = 91.0  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_at_least_one_hot_streak_directors  = 82.0  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_at_least_one_hot_streak_scientists = 90.0  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_one_hot_streak_only_artists   = 64.0  # PAPER_REPORTED (among those with at least one)
RESULT percent_one_hot_streak_only_directors  = 80.0  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_one_hot_streak_only_scientists = 68.0  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_two_hot_streaks_artists   ≈ 30.0  # PAPER_REPORTED (approx)
RESULT percent_two_hot_streaks_directors  = 11.0  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT percent_two_hot_streaks_scientists ≈ 30.0  # PAPER_REPORTED (approx)
RESULT median_tau_H_artists   = 7.3 years  # PAPER_REPORTED (Fig. captions: median 7.3)
RESULT median_tau_H_directors  = 7.0 years  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT median_tau_H_scientists = 4.8 years  # PAPER_REPORTED
NOTE: The main text mentions peaks around 5.7 (artists), 5.2 (directors), 3.7 (scientists) years.
RESULT median_tauH_over_T_artists   = 0.17  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT median_tauH_over_T_directors  = 0.23  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT median_tauH_over_T_scientists = 0.20  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT linear_coefficient_GammaH_vs_Gamma0_artists   = 0.99  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT linear_coefficient_GammaH_vs_Gamma0_directors  = 0.85  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT linear_coefficient_GammaH_vs_Gamma0_scientists = 0.90  # PAPER_REPORTED
RESULT Delta_Gamma_decreases_with_Gamma0  # PAPER_REPORTED (insets)
RESULT KS_test_p_value > 0.05  # PAPER_REPORTED (cannot reject null that NH distribution equals null)
RESULT fraction_within_envelope_peaks_near_1.0  # PAPER_REPORTED (hot-streak model)
RESULT MAPE_improvement_early_onset_most_pronounced  # PAPER_REPORTED (Fig. 3g)
RESULT N_up_over_N_uniformly_distributed  # PAPER_REPORTED (Fig. 2g-i)

CONCLUSION: The paper finds that hot streaks are ubiquitous, temporally localized, and drive collective impact.
The hot-streak model (two-level Γ with a single hot streak) reproduces all observed temporal correlations,
while alternative hypotheses cannot fully account for the empirical patterns.
